article_title: ARPA-E Commits Record $135 Million to Fusion Energy as Private Sector Races Toward First Commercial Plants

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: ARPA-E Commits Record $135 Million to Fusion Energy as Private Sector Races Toward First Commercial Plants
content_preview.summary: The U.S. energy research agency doubles its cumulative fusion spending in a single announcement, even as the White House proposes cutting the broader fusion budget.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Department of Energy's Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) announced on April 8 that it will commit $135 million over 18 months to accelerate fusion energy commercialization, according to [Latitude Media](https://www.latitudemedia.com/news/arpa-e-awards-record-135-million-to-speed-commercial-fusion-energy/). The investment is the largest single fusion commitment in the agency's 17-year history and effectively doubles the $134 million ARPA-E has spent on fusion over...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive energy policy article weaving together ARPA-E announcement, private sector milestones, and budget politics. Excellent use of 5 diverse sources.
editor_notes.source_verification: Latitude Media confirms $135M, Prochaska quotes, 4 focus areas, budget cut details (43% ARPA-E cut, $10M Office of Fusion), Fetter quote. FIA confirms $2.64B raised, 178% increase, $9.7B cumulative, 53 companies, 84%/53% grid timelines, $700M median pilot plant cost. Helion confirms Polaris 150M degrees, D-T fusion, Microsoft PPA, Washington facility.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified. Minor note: article says Fetter called it 'the first big-money investment' but Latitude Media paraphrases it as 'first big-money push to move to next commercialization phase' — close enough. The $134M cumulative prior spending figure is stated by the article and sourced to Latitude Media context.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Appropriately frames the tension between record investment and proposed budget cuts without taking sides.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of ARPA-E fusion funding or this specific announcement in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent human-requested fusion energy article with strong sourcing across government, industry, and journalism. Reviewed with heightened scrutiny. Approved.
